text: Arkansas replaced nurse assessment logic with a computerized RUGs allocation model that reduced home-care hours for many Medicaid beneficiaries. Litigation alleged the system failed to account for individualized conditions and was implemented without proper rulemaking or due process.
ai_system_name: Resource Utilization Groups (RUGs) computerized care-allocation algorithm
canonical_source_conflicted: Medicaid home- and community-based services rules, state APA procedures, individualized care needs
mitigation_gap: No adequate rulemaking, transparency, individualized validation, or notice process accompanied the algorithmic allocation change.
reliance_or_harm: Disabled beneficiaries lost care hours and challenged the system; state-court rulings and later federal litigation led to relief and reforms.
